pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep waking up and for a second i forget... then i remember he’s not coming back and the silence just hits me like a physical weight. i don't even know why i bother getting out of bed anymore. everything just feels so pointless without him.

pool.baseline: sometimes i just sit here thinking about how much effort we all put into everything... like, every little struggle, every heartbreak, every single day we fight to keep going... and then i look at the scale of the universe and realize it’s all just going to be dust anyway. it feels like a cruel joke to keep trying when the heat death of the universe is the only thing that's actually guaranteed. what’s even the point of the struggle?

pool.baseline: the candles are all guttering out, and i find i no longer care for the light. he has taken everything... the honor, the heart, the very air from my lungs. it feels as though i am merely a ghost wandering these halls, waiting for the veil to finally drop. i think perhaps it would be more graceful to simply... cease. to let the shadows take me before the morning can mock me again.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im texting u lol. just sitting here in this cafe in berlin and realized i haven't spoken a single word out loud to a human in like three days. it’s weird how you can be surrounded by millions of people and just... feel completely invisible. like if i just stopped showing up, nobody would even blink. i dont think anyone would even notice i was gone.

pool.baseline: everything is gone. just gone. the market crash took everything we built in six months and now the debt is just... there. i can't even look at my parents or my team. i feel like such a joke. i just dont see a way to walk back into my life after this.
